name: empty_args_returns_none
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_args_returns_none(args: AbstractArgs)
    requires
        args.len() == 0,
    ensures
        parse_args(args) is None,
{
}
reason: Empty args should satisfy the vacuous forall and return Some, not None

name: output_length_mismatch
code:
proof fn phi_2_output_length_mismatch(args: AbstractArgs)
    requires
        parse_args(args) is Some,
    ensures
        parse_args(args).unwrap().len() != args.len(),
{
}
reason: parse_args maps 1-to-1 over args so output length must equal input length

name: some_contains_invalid_address
code:
proof fn phi_3_some_contains_invalid(args: AbstractArgs, i: int)
    requires
        parse_args(args) is Some,
        0 <= i < args.len(),
    ensures
        !parse_args(args).unwrap()[i].valid_physical_address(),
{
}
reason: When parse_args returns Some, all endpoints must have valid addresses by construction

name: parse_loses_identity
code:
proof fn phi_4_parse_loses_identity(args: AbstractArgs, i: int)
    requires
        parse_args(args) is Some,
        0 <= i < args.len(),
    ensures
        parse_args(args).unwrap()[i].id != args[i],
{
}
reason: Each endpoint's id should equal its corresponding arg since parse_arg_as_end_point wraps the arg directly

name: all_valid_but_none
code:
proof fn phi_5_all_valid_but_none(args: AbstractArgs)
    requires
        args.len() > 0,
        forall |i: int| 0 <= i < args.len() ==> (#[trigger] args[i]).len() < 0x100000,
    ensures
        parse_args(args) is None,
{
}
reason: When every arg has length under the threshold all endpoints are valid so parse_args must return Some
